channel.title: LUPO Blog
channel.description: Inbound sales, qualification, and the AI inbound SDR. From the team building LUPO.

item.title: Inbound SDR Metrics: The Numbers That Predict Pipeline
item.description: Speed to lead, qualification rate, form-to-meeting conversion, and cost per qualified meeting: the inbound SDR metrics that matter, with fifteen years of response-time audits in one table.

item.title: What an Inbound SDR Costs in 2026: Salary, Fully Loaded, and the AI Alternative
item.description: US median SDR pay is about $57k base and $96k total. Fully loaded, one rep costs $110k to $175k a year. The itemized math, the UK numbers, the three costs nobody budgets, and the honest AI comparison.

item.title: Inbound SDR vs Outbound SDR: What's Actually Different
item.description: Inbound SDRs convert the leads a company already has. Outbound SDRs create conversations from nothing. Different lead source, tempo, skills, metrics, and AI tooling.

item.title: An AI Inbound SDR Is Not a Chatbot: 7 Differences That Matter
item.description: A chatbot scripts answers on one channel to deflect. An AI inbound SDR qualifies across web form, email, chat, and phone, scores every lead, writes to the CRM, and books meetings.

item.title: The Best AI Inbound SDR Tools in 2026, an Honest Shortlist
item.description: The five AI tools that actually qualify inbound leads in 2026: Qualified (Piper), LUPO, Conversica, Intercom Fin, and 11x for the outbound gap. Who each one fits, what they cost, and how to choose. Written by a vendor, disclosed throughout.

item.title: Qualified Isn't In-Market: How to Tell a Ready Buyer From a Tyre-Kicker
item.description: A lead can be the right person at the right company and still not be ready to buy. Fit tells you who they are. Buying-intent signals, funding, M&A, leadership hires, headcount growth, tell you whether now is the moment. How to score intent instead of guessing.

item.title: The AI SDR Has Moved Inbound: What Sales Leaders Should Automate First
The Inbound Triage Tax: Why Fast Sales Teams Waste the Most Time
item.description: The faster your team responds, the more painful bad inbound becomes. Vendor pitches, support leakage, and half-engaged form fills steal the attention of trained sellers. The metric that matters is not speed-to-lead. It is speed-to-qualified-action.

item.title: Round Robin Is Not a Routing Strategy
item.description: Round robin distributes work. It does not decide whether the work should reach sales in the first place. Serious inbound routing needs qualification, ownership, territory, availability, escalation, and an audit trail.

item.title: The Rep-Free Buyer Still Needs Sales: How to Route Buyers Without Annoying Them
item.description: Two-thirds of B2B buyers say they prefer a rep-free experience. That is not the same as no sales. It means sales has to show up at the right moment, on the right channel, with context. Why instant callback is sometimes wrong.

item.title: Before You Let an AI Agent Touch Revenue, Check These 7 Things
item.description: Sales leaders are interested in AI, but they are afraid of hallucinations, bad CRM writes, compliance issues, and brand damage. A practical readiness checklist for AI in the inbound funnel: permission boundaries, audit trails, escalation, and what to keep human.
